markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' repeated delays of Grand Theft Auto VI—first from a 2025 window to May 2026, then to November 19, 2026—have extended the pre-release period and heightened anticipation around what cultural or industry milestones could occur beforehand. Traders are weighing near-term catalysts like potential new trailers, major album drops from artists such as Playboi Carti or Rihanna, and tech advancements including GPT-6, alongside broader events like political shifts. The extended timeline amplifies uncertainty in entertainment releases and platform metrics, with historical patterns showing how long hype cycles influence competing projects and chart performance. Key upcoming signals include any summer 2026 marketing updates or chart debuts that could shift momentum before the November launch.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York rape retrial—declared May 15 after jurors deadlocked on the Jessica Mann charge—has reinforced trader consensus around “No Prison Time” at 82.5%. At 74 and already serving a 16-year California sentence while appealing both that verdict and a 2025 Manhattan conviction on one count, the former producer faces mounting procedural fatigue and health concerns that historically limit further incarceration in protracted #MeToo cases. Prosecutors must weigh a potential fourth trial against Weinstein’s existing time served and upcoming sentencing, with appeals and age-related factors creating substantial barriers to additional years. Upcoming decisions on retrying the hung charge and any Manhattan sentencing hearing remain the key near-term catalysts.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 Stanley Cup Final between the Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights, currently tied 1-1, drives the market pricing. Carolina's dominant playoff run, including a 12-1 record entering the series and a 6-0 overtime mark, combined with home-ice advantage in Games 2 and 5, supports their slight edge in trader consensus. Vegas countered with a road victory in Game 1 and a four-game sweep of the Colorado Avalanche in the Western Conference Final, highlighting their defensive structure and recent momentum under coach John Tortorella. Both clubs enter with strong regular-season standings and intact rosters, though the best-of-seven format leaves room for shifts based on goaltending and special teams performance in the remaining games.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 NBA Finals matchup between the New York Knicks and San Antonio Spurs has produced the tightest championship futures pricing, with the Knicks holding a narrow edge after stealing Game 1 on the road. New York's 11-game postseason winning streak, anchored by Jalen Brunson's scoring and playmaking alongside strong defensive contributions from role players, has shifted trader sentiment following their 105-95 victory in San Antonio. The Spurs counter with Victor Wembanyama's generational impact and a young core that reached the Finals for the first time in over a decade, though early series struggles in the backcourt have kept the race competitive heading into Game 2. Home-court advantage and roster depth remain key variables in this closely contested series.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: MegaETH airdrop by...?
events.description: this is a market on MegaETH airdrop

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France share the narrowest edge in the pre-tournament consensus due to their exceptional squad depth, recent major-tournament pedigree, and strong recent form heading into the expanded 48-team field. Spain enters as European champions with an unbeaten streak exceeding two years and emerging talents like Lamine Yamal complementing a balanced roster. France counters with proven knockout experience and elite attackers such as Kylian Mbappé. England, Argentina, Portugal, and Brazil remain tightly grouped behind them, buoyed by attacking firepower and historical pedigree, while the 48-team format and group-stage draw create multiple viable paths for upsets. This tight clustering of implied probabilities reflects the absence of a clear standout and the high parity among contenders.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party as General Secretary, with no verified signs of removal or significant internal challenge through mid-2026. He has actively shaped the 15th Five-Year Plan for 2026-2030, delivered a confident New Year address emphasizing party self-governance and national priorities, and directed policy on energy security and technological self-reliance. Recent Central Committee reshuffles and anti-corruption actions have reinforced elite alignment rather than signaled weakness. Preparations for the 21st Party Congress in 2027 focus on personnel assessments under his direction, consistent with his extended tenure since the 2022 congress. Trader consensus at 93% on "No" reflects the absence of credible succession triggers or disruptions within the resolution window ending December 2026.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: In a wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential nomination contest with no declared candidates, traders assign Gavin Newsom the leading 25.1% implied probability, reflecting his established national profile as California governor and executive experience. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez holds second at 9.4% amid speculation over her progressive base and recent national outreach, while Kamala Harris stands at 7.6% tied to her prior vice presidential role. A crowded field of senators, governors, and other figures trails further behind. Key differentiators include name recognition, ideological positioning, fundraising capacity, and early-state visibility. Support could consolidate around one contender through future primary polling trends, party endorsements, or shifts in voter coalitions as the process advances toward 2028.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: In a wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential nomination contest with no declared candidates, traders assign Gavin Newsom the leading 25.1% implied probability, reflecting his established national profile as California governor and executive experience. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez holds second at 9.4% amid speculation over her progressive base and recent national outreach, while Kamala Harris stands at 7.6% tied to her prior vice presidential role. A crowded field of senators, governors, and other figures trails further behind. Key differentiators include name recognition, ideological positioning, fundraising capacity, and early-state visibility. Support could consolidate around one contender through future primary polling trends, party endorsements, or shifts in voter coalitions as the process advances toward 2028.
